content:
# Galatians Chapter 5 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/GAL_005_page_001.jpg, GAL_005_page_002.jpg

### Freedom in Christ

1. Christ has set us free to live a free life. So take your stand! Never again let anyone put a harness of slavery on you.

2. I, Paul, tell you this: if you are counting on circumcision to make you right with God, then Christ is of no benefit to you at all.

3. I say it again. Every man who allows himself to be circumcised is required to obey every single law in the entire law of Moses.

4. For if you are trying to make yourselves right with God by keeping the law, you have been cut off from Christ! You have fallen away from God's grace.

5. But we who live by the Spirit eagerly wait to receive by faith the righteousness God has promised to us.

6. For when we are united with Christ, neither circumcision nor uncircumcision matters. What matters is faith expressing itself through love.

7. You were running the race so well. Who has held you back from following the truth?

8. It certainly isn't God, for he is the one who called you.

9. This false teaching is like a little yeast that spreads through the whole batch of dough!

10. I am trusting the Lord to keep you from believing false teachings. God will judge that person, whoever he is, who has been confusing you.

11. Dear brothers and sisters, if I were still preaching that you must be circumcised — as some say I do — why am I still being persecuted? If I were no longer preaching salvation through the cross of Christ, no one would be offended.

12. I just wish that those troublemakers who want to mutilate you by circumcision would mutilate themselves.

### Living by the Spirit's Power

13. For you have been called to live in freedom, my brothers and sisters. But don't use your freedom to satisfy your sinful nature. Instead, use your freedom to serve one another in love.

14. For the whole law can be summed up in this one command: "Love your neighbor as yourself."

15. But if you are always biting and devouring one another, watch out! Beware of destroying one another.

16. So I say, let the Holy Spirit guide your lives, and you won't be carrying out the selfish desires of your sinful nature.

17. The sinful nature wants to do evil, which is just the opposite of what the Spirit wants. And the Spirit gives us desires that are the opposite of what the sinful nature desires. These two forces are constantly fighting each other, so you are not free to carry out your good intentions.

18. But when you are directed by the Spirit, you are not under obligation to the law of Moses.

19. When you follow the desires of your sinful nature, the results are very clear: sexual immorality, impurity, lustful pleasures,

20. idolatry, sorcery, hostility, quarreling, jealousy, outbursts of anger, selfish ambition, dissension, division,

21. envy, drunkenness, wild parties, and other sins like these. Let me tell you again, as I have before, that anyone living that sort of life will not inherit the Kingdom of God.

22. But the Holy Spirit produces this kind of fruit in our lives: love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,

23. gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against these things!

24. Those who belong to Christ Jesus have nailed the passions and desires of their sinful nature to his cross and crucified them there.

25. Since we are living by the Spirit, let us follow the Spirit's leading in every part of our lives.

26. Let us not become conceited, or provoke one another, or be jealous of one another.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 1 — "Christ has set us free to live a free life":** This is the thesis statement of the entire letter. Everything Paul has argued — the autobiographical defense, the Abraham argument, the Hagar allegory — has been building to this one declaration. Freedom is not a philosophical abstraction. It is the concrete reality of life no longer determined by circumcision, dietary laws, calendar observance, or any other performance metric.

**Verse 6 — "Faith expressing itself through love":** pistis di' agapēs energoumenē. This is Paul's one-sentence summary of the Christian life. Not faith alone (that would be antinomianism). Not works alone (that would be legalism). Faith that works — but works through love, not through law. Luther called this verse the heart of the epistle.

**Verse 12 — "Mutilate themselves":** Paul is not being metaphorical. He is suggesting that if his opponents think cutting flesh pleases God, they should go all the way and castrate themselves. In the Galatian region, the priests of the goddess Cybele practiced ritual castration. Paul's audience would have heard the local reference immediately. It is the most shocking sentence in the Pauline corpus.

**Verses 22-23 — "The fruit of the Spirit":** Note: "fruit" is singular. These nine qualities are not a checklist — they are a single harvest. You don't get to pick three and skip the rest. They describe what a life looks like when the Spirit, not the flesh, is running the show.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
